articles.title: AI Agent Uncovers 21 Zero-Days in FFmpeg; Chrome Patches Record 429 Bugs

The same week, Google shipped Chrome 149 with patches for 429 security bugs, the most ever in a single release. Only the FFmpeg bugs were found by AI.

articles.title: Cisco Patches CVE-2026-20230 in Unified CM as Exploit Code Goes Public
articles.summary: Cisco has patched a bug in Unified Communications Manager that lets an unauthenticated attacker on the network write files to the box and, from there, climb to root. It is tracked as CVE-2026-20230, and proof-of-concept exploit code is already public. Cisco's PSIRT says it has not seen the flaw used in attacks yet. The PoC shortens that runway. The flaw is a server-side request forgery.

keypoints: Unauthenticated network attacker achieves root access via SSRF
Public exploit code accelerates attack timeline significantly

analysis.title: Critical SSRF in Unified CM
analysis.desc: Server-side request forgery vulnerability allows file writes and privilege escalation to root without authentication.

analysis.title: Exploit Already Public
analysis.desc: Proof-of-concept code reduces window before widespread attacks. PSIRT reports no active exploitation yet.

analysis.title: Urgent Patching Required
analysis.desc: Network-adjacent attackers can exploit immediately. Organizations running Unified CM must update now.

ai_summary.suggested_title: Cisco Unified CM Critical SSRF — Public Exploit Available
ai_summary.bottom_line: Your Cisco Unified CM is now a known target. Patch before someone exploits it.
ai_summary.action_today: Contact Cisco support for CVE-2026-20230 patch. Check Unified CM version in System → Software Versions.

articles.title: UN food agency discloses breach affecting 600,000 Gaza households
articles.summary: The United Nations' World Food Programme (WFP), the world's largest humanitarian organization, revealed over the weekend that its self-registration application (SRA) for Palestine was breached. [...]

keypoints: WFP Palestine registration app breached, 600,000 Gaza households affected
Humanitarian organization exposed beneficiary data through self-registration system

analysis.title: Breach scope and impact
analysis.desc: UN World Food Programme's self-registration application compromised, potentially exposing sensitive data of 600,000 Gaza households relying on humanitarian assistance.

analysis.title: Vulnerable population targeted
analysis.desc: Breach affects displaced persons and civilians dependent on food aid, creating secondary risk of exploitation or fraud targeting already vulnerable populations.

analysis.title: Aid infrastructure vulnerability
analysis.desc: Incident exposes critical gaps in cybersecurity practices at major humanitarian organizations managing sensitive beneficiary information during active crises.

ai_summary.suggested_title: UN Food Agency Breach Exposes 600,000 Gaza Residents
ai_summary.bottom_line: Vulnerable families already facing hunger now have their personal data in criminal hands.
ai_summary.action_today: Check if you registered: WFP website → Account → Change password immediately if account exists

articles.title: New IronWorm malware hits 36 packages in npm supply-chain attack
articles.summary: A new supply-chain attack has infected 36 packages on the Node Package Manager (npm) index with infostealer malware called IronWorm. [...]

keypoints: 36 npm packages compromised with IronWorm infostealer malware
Supply-chain attack targets Node.js developer ecosystem directly

analysis.title: IronWorm malware characteristics
analysis.desc: Infostealer designed to exfiltrate sensitive data from infected systems and developer environments.

analysis.title: npm ecosystem vulnerability
analysis.desc: 36 poisoned packages create backdoor access for attackers targeting JavaScript developers globally.

analysis.title: Immediate mitigation required
analysis.desc: Developers must audit dependencies, remove infected packages, and rotate compromised credentials immediately.

ai_summary.suggested_title: npm Supply-Chain Attack: IronWorm Malware Hits 36 Packages
ai_summary.bottom_line: If you use npm packages, assume your credentials are already exposed. Act now.
ai_summary.action_today: npm list → check for IronWorm-affected packages → npm audit fix → rotate API tokens

articles.title: Claude Code GitHub Action Flaw Let One Malicious Issue Hijack Repositories
articles.summary: A security researcher found a flaw in Anthropic's Claude Code GitHub Action that let an attacker take over vulnerable public repositories running it, with nothing more than a single opened GitHub issue. Because Anthropic's own action repo used the same workflow, a working attack could have pushed malicious code into the action itself and onto the projects downstream that pull it. RyotaK of GMO

keypoints: Single GitHub issue enables complete repository takeover
Anthropic's own action vulnerable to downstream supply chain attack

analysis.title: Vulnerability Mechanism
analysis.desc: Attacker opens malicious GitHub issue triggering Claude Code action to execute arbitrary commands with repository access permissions.

analysis.title: Supply Chain Risk
analysis.desc: Flaw in Anthropic's action repository could inject malicious code into action itself, compromising all downstream projects.

analysis.title: Attack Surface
analysis.desc: Public repositories running Claude Code GitHub Action face immediate takeover risk requiring no authentication or special privileges.

ai_summary.suggested_title: GitHub Action Flaw Lets Single Issue Hijack Repositories
ai_summary.bottom_line: Your repository could be compromised right now by someone opening one GitHub issue.
ai_summary.action_today: GitHub → Settings → Actions → Disable Claude Code Action until patched
